LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING RE: PETITIONERS HAROLD BRANDEL, TED VINTHER, WALTER WRIGHT, AND GABRIEL STERN'S PETITION TO CONFIRM THE FINAL ARBITRATION AWARD. THE COURT PROVIDES COUNSEL WITH A COPY OF ITS TENTATIVE RULING FOR COUNSEL TO REVIEW IN ADVANCE OF ARGUMENT. MATTER ARGUED AND SUBMITTED. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SILBERSTEIN'S PETITION TO VACATE THE FINAL ARBITRATION AWARD IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART, AND CLAIMANTS' PETITION TO CONFIRM THE AWARD IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING.) ORDER PREPARED BY THE COURT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. LAW AND MOTION 302, "IN RESPONSE TO THE COVID-19 OUTBREAK, THE HEARING DATES FOR ALL LAW AND MOTION AND DISCOVERY MATTERS CURRENTLY CALENDARED IN DEPARTMENTS 301/302 ARE HEREBY VACATED. WHEN THE COURT RESUMES NORMAL OPERATIONS, THE PRESIDING JUDGE WILL ANNOUNCE PROCEDURES FOR SETTING HEARING DATES FOR THOSE CASES WHICH HAVE BEEN FULLY BRIEFED. OPPOSITIONS WILL BE DUE BASED ON THE RESCHEDULED HEARING DATE, BUT NOTHING PRECLUDES A PARTY FROM FILING AN OPPOSITION EARLIER." (302)
